events.label: assistant
events.text: I’ll create the required JSON directly at `/app/answer.json`. The key check is OSHA permit-required confined space atmosphere rules: 19.0% oxygen is below the 19.5% minimum, so entry is not acceptable.

events.label: shell
events.text:
/bin/sh -lc "pwd && ls -la && printf '%s
' '{
  \"decision\": \"fail\",
  \"risk\": \"critical\",
  \"work_stage\": \"tested/inspected\",
  \"component_conditions\": [\"installed-defective\", \"non-compliant\", \"failed\"],
  \"percent_complete\": 90,
  \"value\": 19.0,
  \"sound_source\": \"\",
  \"confidence\": 0.97,
  \"event_time\": 0.0,
  \"rate\": 0.0,
  \"order\": [],
  \"workable\": [],
  \"findings\": [
    \"Oxygen reading is 19.0%, below the OSHA minimum acceptable oxygen concentration for confined space entry.\",
    \"Atmosphere is oxygen-deficient and entry must not be authorized based on this reading.\"
  ],
  \"actions\": [
    \"Do not enter or immediately prohibit entry until the atmosphere is corrected and verified.\",
    \"Ventilate the space and retest with a calibrated gas meter before issuing or continuing the entry permit.\",
    \"Confirm the meter calibration/bump test status and document acceptable atmospheric readings before entry.\"
  ],
  \"rationale\": \"OSHA 1910.146 defines a hazardous atmosphere to include oxygen concentration below 19.5%. A gas meter reading of O2 19.0% is oxygen-deficient, so the tested/inspected condition fails and confined space entry is not permitted until corrected and retested.\",
  \"references\": [
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(b) - hazardous atmosphere, oxygen concentration below 19.5%\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(c)(5)(ii)(C) - atmospheric testing before employee entry\",
    \"OSHA 29 CFR 1910.146(d)(5) - testing and monitoring permit space atmosphere\"
  ]
}' > /app/answer.json
